Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-05-099
Dear Mr. Orenstein:
This letter is rn reqponse to your request for advice by way of correspondence
dated May 12, 2005 regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the political Reform
Act (the "Act').I The Act requires the Commission
person whose duties underthe Act are in
to provide written advice to any
question or to that person,s authorized
representative. (Section 831 I4(b); regulation I8329(a) - copy enclosed.) However, the
Commission may decline to provide written advice where the requestor is seeking advice
that requires us to evaluate conduct that has already occurred. @egulation
1832e(bX8XA).) In addition, the Commission may also decline to provide written advice
if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. @egulation
r832e(bx8xF).)
Formal written advice is the application of law to a gr-ven set of facts provided by
the requestor and has the purpose orconrerri"g i*u;tiJtrr","qu"rt* d""tioo
83114; regulation 18329(b[i).) After u "*"fuIreview ortrr" facts you submitted, we
decline to provide *1I* advice to you at this til". y;; current question involves
analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, such
that issuing advice at this time wourd relateio p^t;;;;; 
and, therefore, would not
further the impartial, efifbctive administration oith" Act so mandated by section g3111.
I Govemment code sections 8 1000 - ?1914- commission regulations appear at title 2, sections
18109 - 18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. W-05-099
Page2
Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your
request. In addition, nothing in the Act prevents an official from sleking legaladvice
from you orprivate counsel.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
Andreas
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
